sentences.text: UnitedHealthcare® Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Bariatric Surgery Policy Number: 2026T0362QQ Effective Date: January 1, 2026  Instructions for Use Table of Contents Page Related Commercial/Individual Exchange Policies Application .......................................................................... 1 • Minimally Invasive Procedures for the Treatment Coverage Rationale ............................................................ 1 of Upper Gastrointestinal Diseases Medical Records Documentation Used for Reviews ............. 3 • Obstructive and Central Sleep Apnea Treatment Definitions ........................................................................... 3 Applicable Codes ................................................................ 4 • Robotic-Assisted Surgery Policy, Professional Description of Services........................................................ 5 Community Plan Policy Benefit Considerations ........................................................ 8 • Bariatric Surgery Clinical Evidence ................................................................. 8 U.S. Food and Drug Administration ................................... 41 References ....................................................................... 42 Policy History/Revision Information ................................... 52 Instructions for Use ........................................................... 53 Application UnitedHealthcare Commercial This Medical Policy applies to UnitedHealthcare Commercial benefit plans.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: UnitedHealthcare Individual Exchange This Medical Policy applies to Individual Exchange benefit plans in all states except for Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Indiana, Kansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Nebraska, Ohio, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Washington, and Wisconsin.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Coverage Rationale  See Benefit Considerations The following bariatric surgical procedures are proven and medically necessary for treating obesity: Biliopancreatic diversion/biliopancreatic diversion with duodenal switch Gastric bypass (includes robotic-assisted gastric bypass) Adjustable gastric banding (using open or laparoscopic approaches) for individuals > 18 years of age; refer to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) section for additional information Sleeve gastrectomy (vertical sleeve gastrectomy) In adults age 18 years or older, bariatric surgery using one of the procedures identified above for treating obesity is proven and medically necessary when all of the following criteria are met: One of the following BMI ≥ 40 kg/m2 (or BMI ≥ 37.5 kg/m2 in individuals of Asian descent); or o BMI ≥ 35 kg/m2 – 39.9 kg/m2 (or BMI ≥ 32.5 kg/m2 – 37.4 kg/m2 in individuals of Asian descent) in the presence of o one or more of the following co-morbidities:  Insulin resistance or Type 2 diabetes; or  Cardiovascular disease [e.g., history of stroke and/or myocardial infarction, poorly controlled hypertension (systolic blood pressure greater than 140 mm Hg or diastolic blood pressure 90 mm Hg or greater, despite pharmacotherapy), coronary artery disease, hyperlipidemia]; or Bariatric Surgery Page 1 of 53 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 01/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc.  History of cardiomyopathy; or  Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA) confirmed on polysomnography with an AHI or RDI of > 30; or  Evidence of Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD); or  Idiopathic intracranial hypertension (pseudotumor cerebri) and The individual must also meet the following criteria: Both of the following: o  Completion of a preoperative evaluation that includes a detailed weight history along with dietary and physical activity patterns; and  Psychosocial-behavioral evaluation by an individual who is professionally recognized as part of a behavioral health discipline to provide screening and identification of risk factors or potential postoperative challenges that may contribute to a poor postoperative outcome or Participation in a Multidisciplinary surgical preparatory regimen o In adolescents age 12-17 years, the bariatric surgical procedures identified above are proven and medically necessary for treating obesity when all of the following criteria are met: One of the following: Class III obesity; or o Class II obesity in the presence of one or more of the following co-morbidities: o  Insulin resistance or Type 2 diabetes; or  Poorly controlled hypertension (systolic blood pressure greater than 140 mm Hg or diastolic blood pressure 90 mm Hg or greater, despite pharmacotherapy); or  Hyperlipidemia; or  Obstructive Sleep Apnea confirmed on polysomnography with an AHI or RDI of > 30; or  Evidence of Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD); or  Idiopathic intracranial hypertension (pseudotumor cerebri) and The individual must also receive an evaluation at, or in consultation with, a Multidisciplinary center focused on the surgical treatment of severe childhood obesity; this may include Adolescent centers that have received accreditation by the Metabolic and Bariatric Surgery Accreditation and Quality Improvement Program (MBSAQIP) or can demonstrate similar programmatic components A planned two-stage procedure is proven and medically necessary when all of the following criteria are met: Initial BMI ≥ 50 kg/m2 prior to first stage bariatric procedure; and Second stage occurs within 2 years following the primary bariatric surgery procedure; and Individual has been compliant with nutrition and exercise; and Individual meets medical necessity criteria listed above at time of second stage procedure Revisional Bariatric Surgery using one of the procedures identified above is proven and medically necessary when due to a technical failure or major complication from the initial procedure; potential failure/complications include but are not limited to the following: Bowel perforation (including adjustable gastric band erosion) Adjustable gastric band migration (slippage) that cannot be corrected with manipulation or adjustment (records must demonstrate that manipulation or adjustment to correct band slippage has been attempted) Leak Obstruction (confirmed by imaging studies) Staple-line failure Mechanical adjustable gastric band failure Uncontrollable reflux related to sleeve gastrectomy when all the following criteria are met: Maximum nonpharmacological medical management failure (e.g., positional, dietary modification, and behavioral o changes); and Maximum pharmacological medical management failure (e.g., at least one month of double dose PPI, H2 blocker, o and/or sucralfate); and Severe esophagitis (grade C or D) confirmed by endoscopy despite maximum medical management o Removal of adjustable gastric band and all related components which does not result in a revisional surgery is proven and medically necessary.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc. The following procedures are unproven and not medically necessary for treating obesity due to insufficient evidence of efficacy: Revisional Bariatric Surgery for any other indication than those listed above Bariatric surgery as the primary treatment for any condition other than obesity Bariatric interventions for the treatment of obesity including but not limited to: Bariatric artery embolization (BAE) o Gastric electrical stimulation with an implantable gastric stimulator (IGS) o Intragastric balloon o Laparoscopic greater curvature plication, also known as total gastric vertical plication o Mini-gastric bypass (MGB)/laparoscopic mini-gastric bypass (LMGBP)/one-anastomosis gastric bypass (OAGB) o Single-anastomosis duodenal switch [also known as duodenal switch with single anastomosis or stomach o intestinal pylorus sparing surgery (SIPS)] Stomach aspiration therapy o Transoral endoscopic surgery [includes TransPyloric Shuttle® (TPS®) device, endoscopic sleeve gastroplasty] o Vagus nerve blocking (VBLOC®) o Gastrointestinal liners o Medical Records Documentation Used for Reviews Benefit coverage for health services is determined by the member specific benefit plan document and applicable laws that may require coverage for a specific service.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Medical records documentation may be required to assess whether the member meets the clinical criteria for coverage but does not guarantee coverage of the service requested; refer to the guidelines titled Medical Records Documentation Used for Reviews.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Definitions Asian: Refers to a person having origins from the Far East, Southeast Asia, or the Indian subcontinent (e.g., Cambodia, China, India, Japan, Korea, Malaysia, Pakistan, the Philippine Islands, Thailand, and Vietnam) (United States Census Bureau, 2012).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Body Mass Index (BMI): A person's weight in kilograms divided by the square of height in meters.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: BMI can be used as a screening tool but is not diagnostic of the body fatness or health of an individual [Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), 2017].
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute’s (NHLBI) Practical Guide Identification, Evaluation and Treatment of Overweight and Obesity in Adults classifies the ranges of BMI in adults as follows: 40 kg/m2 – Obesity Class III The American Society of Metabolic and Bariatric Surgeons (ASMBS; Pratt et al.,
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2018), classifies severe obesity in adolescents as follows: Class II obesity – 120% of the 95th percentile height, or an absolute BMI of 35-39.9 kg/m2, whichever is lower* Class III obesity – 140% of the 95th percentile height, or an absolute BMI of > 40 kg/m2, whichever is lower *Also as defined by the American Heart Association (Kelly et al., 2013).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Los Angeles (LA) Classification of Oesophagitis: Grade A: One (or more) mucosal break no longer than 5 mm that does not extend between the tops of two mucosal folds Grade B: One (or more) mucosal break more than 5 mm long that does not extend between the tops of two mucosal folds Grade C: One (or more) mucosal break that is continuous between the tops of two or more mucosal folds but which involve less than 75% of the circumference Grade D: One (or more) mucosal break which involves at least 75% of the esophageal circumference (Lundell et al.,
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 1999) Bariatric Surgery Page 3 of 53 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 01/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc. Multidisciplinary: Bariatric center or regimen combining or involving several academic disciplines or professional specializations in an approach to create a well-trained, safe, and effective environment for the complex bariatric patient.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Building the Multidisciplinary team includes staff such as the bariatric surgeon, obesity medicine specialist, registered dietician, specialized nursing, behavioral health specialist, exercise specialist and support groups [American Society for Metabolic and Bariatric Surgery (ASMBS) textbook of bariatric surgery].
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD): Condition that is evidenced by hepatic steatosis (HS) diagnosed either by imaging or histology without a secondary cause of hepatic fat accumulation such as significant alcohol consumption, long- term use of steatogenic medication, or monogenic hereditary disorders (Chalasani et al., 2018).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA): The American Academy of Sleep Medicine (AASM) defines OSA as a sleep related breathing disorder that involves a decrease or complete halt in airflow despite an ongoing effort to breathe.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: OSA severity is defined as: Mild for AHI or RDI ≥ 5 and 30/hour For additional information, refer to the Medical Policy titled Obstructive and Central Sleep Apnea Treatment.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Revisional Bariatric Surgery: Conversion – A second bariatric procedure that changes the bariatric approach from one procedure to a different type of procedure (e.g., sleeve gastrectomy or adjustable gastric band converted to Roux-en-Y [RYGB]).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Note: This is not the same as an intraoperative conversion (e.g., converting from laparoscopic approach to an open procedure).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Corrective – A procedure that corrects or modifies anatomy of a previous bariatric procedure to achieve the original desired outcome or correct a complication.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: These procedures also address device manipulation (e.g., gastric pouch resizing, re-sleeve gastrectomy, limb length adjustments in RYGB and gastric band replacement).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Reversal – A procedure that restores original anatomy (Mirkin et al.,
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2021) Applicable Codes The following list(s) of procedure and/or diagnosis codes is provided for reference purposes only and may not be all inclusive.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Coding Clarification: Utilize CPT code 43775 to report laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy rather than the unlisted CPT code 43659.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: CPT Code Description 0813T Esophagogastroduodenoscopy, flexible, transoral, with volume adjustment of intragastric bariatric balloon 43290 Esophagogastroduodenoscopy, flexible, transoral; with deployment of intragastric bariatric balloon 43291 Esophagogastroduodenoscopy, flexible, transoral; with removal of intragastric bariatric balloon(s) 43644 Laparoscopy, surgical, gastric restrictive procedure; with gastric bypass and Roux-en-Y gastroenterostomy (roux limb 150 cm or less) 43645 Laparoscopy, surgical, gastric restrictive procedure; with gastric bypass and small intestine reconstruction to limit absorption 43647 Laparoscopy, surgical; implantation or replacement of gastric neurostimulator electrodes, antrum 43648 Laparoscopy, surgical; revision or removal of gastric neurostimulator electrodes, antrum 43659 Unlisted laparoscopy procedure, stomach 43770 Laparoscopy, surgical, gastric restrictive procedure; placement of adjustable gastric restrictive device (e.g., gastric band and subcutaneous port components) 43771 Laparoscopy, surgical, gastric restrictive procedure; revision of adjustable gastric restrictive device component only Bariatric Surgery Page 4 of 53 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 01/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.

removal and replacement of adjustable gastric restrictive device component only 43774 Laparoscopy, surgical, gastric restrictive procedure; removal of adjustable gastric restrictive device and subcutaneous port components 43775 Laparoscopy, surgical, gastric restrictive procedure; with short limb (150 cm or less) Roux-en-Y gastroenterostomy 43847 Gastric restrictive procedure, with gastric bypass for morbid obesity; with small intestine reconstruction to limit absorption 43848 Revision, open, of gastric restrictive procedure for morbid obesity, other than adjustable gastric restrictive device (separate procedure) 43860 Revision of gastrojejunal anastomosis (gastrojejunostomy) with reconstruction, with or without partial gastrectomy or intestine resection; without vagotomy 43865 Revision of gastrojejunal anastomosis (gastrojejunostomy) with reconstruction, with or without partial gastrectomy or intestine resection; with vagotomy 43881 Implantation or replacement of gastric neurostimulator electrodes, antrum, open 43882 Revision or removal of gastric neurostimulator electrodes, antrum, open 43886 Gastric restrictive procedure, open; revision of subcutaneous port component only 43887 Gastric restrictive procedure, open; removal of subcutaneous port component only 43888 Gastric restrictive procedure, open; removal and replacement of subcutaneous port component only 43889 Gastric restrictive procedure, transoral, endoscopic sleeve gastroplasty (ESG), including argon plasma coagulation, when performed 43999 Unlisted procedure, stomach 64590 Insertion or replacement of peripheral, sacral, or gastric neurostimulator pulse generator or receiver, requiring pocket creation and connection between electrode array and pulse generator or receiver 64595 Revision or removal of peripheral, sacral, or gastric neurostimulator pulse generator or receiver, with detachable connection to electrode array 64999 Unlisted procedure, nervous system CPT® is a registered trademark of the American Medical Association Description of Services Obesity Obesity is defined clinically using the Body Mass Index (BMI).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Obesity is a significant health concern due to its high prevalence and associated health risks.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Health consequences associated with obesity include hypertension, Type II diabetes, hyperlipidemia, atherosclerosis, heart disease, stroke, diseases of the gallbladder, liver disease, osteoarthritis, Obstructive Sleep Apnea, and other respiratory problems.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In addition, certain cancers are more prevalent in obese individuals, including endometrial, ovarian, breast, prostate, colon cancer, renal cell carcinoma, and non-Hodgkin's lymphoma.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The U.S. Preventive Services Task Force (USPSTF) recommends screening all adults for obesity.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Clinicians should offer or refer patients with a BMI of 30 kg/m2 or higher to intensive, multicomponent behavioral interventions (USPSTF, 2012).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Bariatric Surgery Page 5 of 53 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 01/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc. Bariatric Surgery in the Adolescent Population For Adolescents, physical development and maturation may be determined utilizing the gender specific growth chart and BMI chart developed by the CDC, National Center for Health Statistics.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: First-Line Treatments for Obesity First-line treatments for obesity include dietary therapy, physical activity, behavior modification, and medication management; all of which have often been unsuccessful in long-term weight management for obese individuals (Lannoo and Dillemans, 2014).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Bariatric Surgical Procedures The goal of surgical treatment for obesity is to induce significant weight loss and, thereby, reduce the incidence or progression of obesity-related comorbidities, as well as to improve quality of life.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The purpose of performing bariatric surgery in Adolescent individuals is to reduce the lifelong impact of severe obesity.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Surgical treatment of obesity offers two main weight-loss approaches: restrictive and malabsorptive.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Restrictive methods are intended to cause weight loss by restricting the amount of food that can be consumed by reducing the size of the stomach.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Malabsorptive methods are intended to cause weight loss by limiting the amount of food that is absorbed from the intestines into the body.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: A procedure can have restrictive features, malabsorptive features, or both.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The surgical approach can be open or laparoscopic.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The clinical decision on which surgical procedure to use is made based on a medical assessment of the patient's unique situation.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Roux-en-y Bypass (RYGB)/Gastric Bypass The RYGB procedure involves creating a stomach pouch out of a small portion of the stomach and attaching it directly to the small intestine, bypassing a large part of the stomach and duodenum.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Laparoscopic Adjustable Gastric Banding (LAGB) The laparoscopic adjustable gastric banding procedure involves placing an inflatable silicone band around the upper portion of the stomach.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The silicone band contains a saline reservoir that can be filled or emptied under fluoroscopic guidance to change the caliber of the gastric opening.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Vertical Sleeve Gastrectomy (VSG) VSG can be performed as part of a two-staged approach to surgical weight loss or as a stand-alone procedure.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: A VSG involves the removal of 60-75% of the stomach, leaving a narrow gastric “tube” or “sleeve.”
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: This small remaining “tube” cannot hold as much food and produces less of the appetite-regulating hormone ghrelin, lessening a patient’s desire to eat.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: VSG is not a purely malabsorptive procedure, so there is no requirement for lifetime nutritional supplementation (California Technology Assessment Forum, 2015).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Biliopancreatic Diversion With Duodenal Switch (BPD/DS) (Also Known as the Scopinaro Procedure) BPD is primarily malabsorptive but has a temporary restrictive component.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: As in RYGB, three "limbs" of intestine are created: one through which food passes, one that permits emptying of fluids (e.g., bile) from digestive organs, and a common limb through which both food and digestive fluids pass.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: This procedure involves removal of the greater curvature of the stomach instead of the distal portion.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The two limbs meet in a common channel measuring only 50 to 100 cm, thereby permitting relatively little absorption.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Robotic-Assisted Surgery Robotic surgery provides surgeons with three-dimensional vision, increased dexterity and precision by downscaling surgeon's movements enabling a fine tissue dissection and filtering out physiological tremor.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: It overcomes the restraint of torque on ports from thick abdominal wall and minimizes port site trauma by remote center technology (Bindal et al., 2015).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Transoral Endoscopic Surgery Transoral endoscopic surgery is an option being explored for bariatric surgery.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Natural orifice transluminal endoscopic surgery (NOTES) is performed via a natural orifice (e.g., mouth, vagina, etc.),
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: and in some cases eliminates the need for abdominal incisions.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: This form of surgery is being investigated as an alternative to conventional surgery.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Bariatric Surgery Page 6 of 53 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 01/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc. Transoral restorative obesity surgery (ROSE) is another endoscopic procedure.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The endoscope with four channels is inserted into the esophagus and then the stomach.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Specialized instruments are placed through the channels to create multiple folds around the existing stoma to reduce the diameter.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The Transpyloric Shuttle® (TPS®) device is a non-balloon, space occupying device with a 12-month treatment duration that is proposed as a new endoscopic bariatric therapy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The TPS device is comprised of a spherical silicone bulb connected to a smaller cylindrical silicone bulb by a flexible tether; it is delivered to and removed from the stomach using transluminal endoscopic procedures in the outpatient setting (Marinos, 2014).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The device was granted FDA premarket approval on April 16, 2019, and was approved for up to 12 months weight loss therapy in individuals with a BMI of 35.0 kg/m2 to 40.0 kg/m2 or a BMI of 30.0 kg/m2 to 34.9 kg/m2 with 1 or more obesity-related comorbid condition.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The device is intended to be used in conjunction with a diet and behavior modification program (ECRI, 2019).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Endoscopic Sleeve Gastroplasty (ESG) is a minimally invasive technique through the mouth that uses an endoscopic suturing device (e.g., OverStitch) to reduce gastric capacity by sealing off most of the stomach, forcing ingested food through an open tube of stomach tissue that connects the esophagus to the small intestine.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: ESG is similar to a laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy in which the stomach is manipulated to create a tube-shape, however no stomach tissue is removed.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Laparoscopic Mini Gastric Bypass (LMGBP)/One-Anastomosis Gastric Bypass (OAGB) LMGBP/OAGB involves the construction of a gastric tube by dividing the stomach vertically, down to the antrum.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: As in the RYGB, food does not enter the distal stomach.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: However, unlike gastric bypass surgery, digestive enzymes and bile are not diverted away from the stomach after LMGBP/OAGB.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: This can lead to bile reflux gastritis which can cause pain that is difficult to treat.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Implantable Gastric Stimulator (IGS) IGS is a small, battery-powered device similar to a cardiac pacemaker, in a small pocket, created beneath the skin of the abdomen using laparoscopy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Prior to revisional surgery, individuals should undergo a thorough Multidisciplinary assessment and consideration of their individual risks and benefits from revisional surgery (Brethauer et al., 2014).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: It is important to determine if the poor response to primary bariatric surgery is due to anatomic causes that led to inadequate weight loss or weight regain or to the patient’s postoperative behavior, such as not following the prescribed diet and lifestyle changes (e.g., consuming large portions, high-calorie foods, and/or snacks between meals; not exercising).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Uncontrollable reflux may be a complication experienced by some individuals; first-line therapy for individuals who experience GERD after bariatric surgery includes dietary and lifestyle modification, alcohol, and smoking cessation, followed by acid-reducing medications (King et al., 2021).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Clinical Evidence In a 2022 systematic review and meta-analysis, Alghamdi compared the results of Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (LRYGB) and laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy (LSG) on the obesity comorbidities of diabetes, hypertension, dyslipidemias, obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) and GERD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Sixteen randomized controlled trials in adults with a BMI > 27.5 kg/m2 with a follow up of 1-5 years were included (no trials with revision or conversion procedures were included).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results showed that for diabetes remission, 14 trials reported no statistically significant differences.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There were statistically significant differences for hypertension (8 trials) and dyslipidemia (5 trials) remission in favor of LRYGB with substantial heterogeneity for dyslipidemia seen.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Obstructive sleep apnea was assessed by 4 trials and showed equal efficacy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Two trials reported on GERD and showed that LRYGB was better for remission but there were no significant differences with regard to new onset GERD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Surgical complications were assessed and showed that LSG is associated with fewer early and late complications including dumping syndrome and fewer nutritional deficiencies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There was inconsistent reporting on the effects of the procedures on gut hormones and liver enzymes that contribute to nonalcoholic fatty liver disease.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Bariatric Surgery Page 8 of 53 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 01/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Copyright 2026 United HealthCare Services, Inc. The authors concluded that the results of the two procedures are similar with LRYGB showing superiority to LSG for GERD, hypertension, and dyslipidemia remission. Khalaj et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2020) conducted a cohort study comparing gastric bypass (GB) to sleeve gastrectomy (SG) and the effectiveness and safety of these two procedures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors evaluated 2,202 patients that underwent laparoscopic SG and 1,085 patients who underwent laparoscopic GB.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The SG procedure was performed over a 36-F bougie and reinforced with an omental pouch; the GB procedure was performed as either RYGB or one anastomosis (OAGB).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Evaluation of weight loss included body mass index change, percent of total weight loss, and percentage of excess weight loss.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM), hypertension (HTN), and dyslipidemia, as obesity-associated comorbidities were assessed in all patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There were no major complications identified which was recognized by a return to the operating room, prolonged hospital stays beyond 7 days, or the need for re-admission.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Quality of life (QoL) was assessed using the Iranian version of the Short-Form Health Survey which measured physical, social, and mental aspects of health.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patient follow up for both types of procedures occurred at 6, 12, and 24 months after surgery.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors found no significant differences between the two surgical groups; patients that underwent SG had a lower FPG and HbA1C when compared to the GB group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: BMI was not significantly different between the two groups.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Excess weight loss (EWL)% was 61.9 ±15.7, 74.8 ±19.1, and 75.0 ±21.9 in the SG group and 62.7 ±15.3, 77.5 ±18.4, and 80.1 ±20.8 in the GB group at 6-, 12-, and 24-month follow-ups, respectively.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: All patient comorbidities and QoL improved.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that bariatric surgery is effective and safe for treatment of obesity; while both procedures are effective for weight loss, remission of obesity-associated comorbidities, and QoL, SG is associated with fewer complications and nutritional deficiencies. Jung et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2020) conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of 22 studies with 2,141 patients to comprehensively evaluate the efficacy of different endoscopic bariatric procedures compared to lifestyle modification in the treatment of morbid obesity.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Intragastric balloon, duodenal-jejunal bypass liner (DJBL), aspiration therapy, primary obesity surgery endoluminal (POSE) procedure, and botulinum toxin injection to the stomach were included and the meta-analysis determined the percentage of weight loss (%weight loss) and percentage of excess weight loss (%EWL).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results showed that the Obalon Balloon system was shown to have efficacy for both %weight loss and %EWL, its efficacy was not proven due to the small number of studies and comparatively low effect size.

sentences.text: Aspiration therapy demonstrated effectiveness for weight reduction when compared to lifestyle modification.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Gas-filled balloon and botulinum toxin injection did not show a significant difference in %weight loss or %EWL compared with the control.

sentences.text: The authors concluded that all bariatric endoscopic procedures, with the exception of a gas filled balloon and botulinum toxin injection show superior short-term efficacy compared with lifestyle modification.

sentences.text: 2019) performed a systematic review and meta-analysis on 33 reports containing ten or more years of follow-up for patients that underwent bariatric surgery.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors evaluated the long-term effectiveness of Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (RYGB), laparoscopic adjustable gastric banding (LAGB), or BPD/DS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Results for gastric bypass surgery showed a weighted mean % EWL of 56.7% at 10 or more years with a mean of 55.4% EWL.

sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A longitudinal cohort study for the patients receiving LAGB showed patient weight loss reached a peak at the 2-year follow- up and remained relatively stable through the next 18 years with a mean weight loss of 24.8 kg representing 47.2 %EWL.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that RYGB, LAGB and BPD/DS lead to substantial weight loss which continued for at least 10 years.

sentences.text: Due to patient education and lap band design changes, revisional surgery has decreased significantly over the past eleven years.

sentences.text: The findings are limited by lack of direct comparison between techniques and lack of comparison groups not undergoing surgical treatments. (

sentences.text: Zhao and Jiao (2019) conducted a systematic review to determine whether LRYGB and LSG are equivalent for mid- and long-term weight loss, resolution of comorbidities and adverse events (AEs).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Eleven RCTs were included in the meta- analysis and the authors found no significant difference in excess weight loss between LRYGB and LSG nor any significant difference for T2D improvement.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This analysis did identify more postoperative early complications for LRYGB, but no difference between the two procedures in later postoperative period.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Future studies should focus on the comparison of complication and comorbidities.

sentences.text: Limitations included the variation in sample size among the included studies which may have created a bias, variation of patient age and preoperative BMIs which may have led to heterogeneity, and failure of subgroup analysis for reoperation rate.

sentences.text: The RYGB group experienced greater total body weight loss at 6, 12, and 24 months (41.9 vs. 34.6 kg at 24 months, p 0.99), bleed (0% versus 0.3%, p > 0.99), reoperation (1.2% versus .6%, p > .99), or readmission (3% versus 1.2%, p = .45). There were no mortalities.

sentences.text: After matching for age, sex, and BMI, the authors found no significant differences between BPD/DS and SG with regard to 30-day postoperative rates of leak, bleed, reoperation, readmission, or mortality. Risstad et al. (

sentences.text: 2017) conducted a randomized clinical trial with 60 patients with body mass index 50-60 kg/m2 to investigate bile acid profiles up to 5 years after RYGB and BPD/DS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Total bile acid concentrations increased substantially over 5 years after both RYGB and BPD/DS, with greater increases in total and primary bile acids after BPD/DS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Higher levels of total bile acids at 5 years were associated with lower body mass index, greater weight loss, and lower total cholesterol. Xie et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2016) prospectively evaluated Apnea-Hypopnea Index (AHI) and Functional Outcomes of Sleep Questionnaires Scores (FOSQ) pre- and post-operatively in patients undergoing bariatric surgery. A total of 167 subjects were studied.

sentences.text: The median age was 46 (14-75) years and BMI 49 (36-69) kg/m2.

sentences.text: 2015) assessed weight loss and occurrence of weight regain among patients (n = 300) at 1 year follow-up who underwent RYGB at a single institution.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The mean weight regain for all patients was 23.4 % of maximum weight loss.

sentences.text: Robotic-Assisted Gastric Bypass Surgery Leang et al. (
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2024) conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis to evaluate the peri-operative outcomes of patients with obesity undergoing robotic gastric bypass versus laparoscopic gastric bypass surgery.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Twenty-eight studies (1 RCT, 1 prospective study, and 26 retrospective studies) comprised of 82,155 individuals were included.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Robotic bypass surgery (RBS) was performed on 9051participants versus 73,104 laparoscopic bypass surgery (LBS).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Outcomes assessed included overall and intraoperative complication rates, anastomotic leak and stricture, surgical site infection, reoperation rates within 30 days.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Mortality, and hospital length of stay (LOS).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Secondary outcomes included operative time, estimated blood loss, conversion, and readmission rates.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results showed that of the 26 articles reporting overall complications, there were no differences.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In the 10 that reported intraoperative complications, there were also no differences between the procedures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There were no significant differences seen in anastomotic leak or stricture, surgical site infection or hospital length of stay.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Mortality was not statistically different in the 26 articles reporting on this outcome.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The reoperation rate within 30 days was reported by 16 articles and showed RBS with a higher rate of 4.4% compared to 3.4% for LBS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Secondary outcomes also showed no significant differences, but there was heterogeneity among reporting this outcome.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that there are no significant differences in outcomes between the two procedures, with RBS findings at risk of bias.

sentences.text: The Teen-Longitudinal Assessment of Bariatric Surgery (LABS) Study was a prospective, multicenter, observational study, which enrolled 242 adolescents (≤ 19 years of age) who were undergoing bariatric surgery from March 2007 through February 2012 at 5 U.S. adolescent bariatric surgery centers.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The patients underwent RYGB (n = 161), SG (n = 67), or LAGB (n = 14). Ryder et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2016) evaluated 2-year outcomes to determine the impact of bariatric surgery on functional mobility and musculoskeletal pain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Participants completed a 400-m walk test prior to bariatric surgery (n = 206) and at 6 months (n = 195), 12 months (n = 176), and 24 months (n = 149) after surgery.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Time to completion, resting heart rate (HR), immediate posttest HR, and HR difference (resting HR minus posttest HR) were measured and musculoskeletal pain concerns, during and after the test, were documented.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Data were adjusted for age, sex, race/ethnicity, baseline BMI, and surgical center (posttest HR and HR difference were further adjusted for changes in time to completion).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Compared with the baseline, the post-surgery data showed an improvement in all measurements at all times measured.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors conclude that bariatric surgery in adolescents with extreme obesity is associated with significant improvement in functional mobility and in the reduction of walking-related musculoskeletal pain up to 2 years after surgery.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Findings are however limited by lack of comparison group.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Bariatric Artery Embolization (BAE) There is insufficient evidence for bariatric artery embolization and its outcomes for weight loss; additional robust RCTs are warranted for safety and efficacy along with long-term follow up. Reddy et al. (
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: 2020) conducted a single-center, sham controlled, masked RCT to evaluate the efficacy of transcatheter bariatric embolization (TBE) for weight reduction in obesity.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Participants were randomized to either sham procedure (n = 20) or TBE targeting the left gastric artery using embolic beads (n = 20).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The primary efficacy endpoint was the difference in TBWL between the two groups at 6 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: All patients entered a lifestyle counseling program and follow-up was completed by physicians that were masked to allocated therapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: At 6 months, the TBWL for TBE in the intention to treat (ITT) population was 7.4 kg compared to 3.0 kg for sham procedure.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The change in BMI at 6 months for ITT was -2.6 in TBE versus -1.1 in sham.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The TBE ITT population did maintain the weight loss at 12 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Patients within the sham group were unblinded at 6 months and permitted to crossover to TBE and then only initial group was followed for 12 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Limitations included small sample size, single center, no control group after 6 months, and possibility that the efficacy of TBE was related to subject participation in weight management counseling as it is unknown if TBE alone would have an impact on obesity without lifestyle counseling.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Additionally, four subjects withdrew consent after randomization and another three prior to the 6-month visit.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Furthermore, the clinical significance of the effect, its long-term sustainability, and safety are unclear. Hafezi-Nejad et al. (
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2019) conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of case series investigating the safety and efficacy of left gastric artery (LGA) embolization as a bariatric procedure.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Meta-regression was performed to assess associations of age, sex, body mass index, and ghrelin and leptin levels with weight change after LGA embolization were selected.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Six case series published between January 2014 and April 2019, comprising 47 patients investigating the safety and/or efficacy of LGA embolization for weight loss were included in the meta-analysis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results showed a mean weight loss of 8.68 kg (19.14 lbs.)
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: after 12 months of follow-up, approximately 8% of baseline total body weight which is superior to weight loss from diet and exercise, and comparable to other more invasive interventions.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Transient superficial mucosal ulcers were common after LGA embolization, and one case of major complications (severe pancreatitis, splenic infarct, and gastric perforation) was identified.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There were considerable variations in patient age, sex distribution, and baseline characteristics among the studies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Significant variation was observed in the duration of follow-up, which ranged from 3 months to 20–24 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Limitations of this study include variations in the indications for LGA embolization, study designs, embolization techniques, follow-up plans, dietary assessments, patient comorbidities, and availability of control subjects, the authors concluded that LGA embolization is an investigative method and not yet proven to be effective management for obesity.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Larger studies are needed to expand these findings and determine other correlates of weight Bariatric Surgery Page 19 of 53 UnitedHealthcare Commercial and Individual Exchange Medical Policy Effective 01/01/2026 Proprietary Information of UnitedHealthcare.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Publications by Bai 2018, Syed 2016, and Weiss 2017, which were previously cited in this policy, are included in this systematic review). Weiss et al. (
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2019) evaluated the safety and efficacy of bariatric artery embolization up to twelve months following surgery in 20 severely obese patients (five of which are identified below in the Weiss et al. ( 2017) case series).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The primary endpoint was weight loss with additional end points assessed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Bariatric embolization was performed successfully in all participants.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Participants experienced mean excess weight loss of 8.2% at one month, 11.5% at 3 months, 12.8% at six months and 11.5% at twelve months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The mean total weight loss was 7.6kg at twelve months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: As a result of loss to follow- up, 18 participants remained at three months, 16 at six months, and 15 at twelve months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: No major adverse events (AE) were identified and only eleven minor AE occurred in eight participants.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors found bariatric embolization is well tolerated and promotes clinically relevant weight loss in adults with severe obesity.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Limitations included lack of comparison group, small sample size, insufficient data due to lack of continuous follow up for several participants, required weight management compliance before the embolization procedure on the first five participants only and a large portion of participants were African American thus overrepresenting that population.

sentences.text: Gastric Electrical Stimulator (GES) While gastric electrical stimulation may provide benefit for obesity, additional well designed RCTs with long-term follow-up are warranted to demonstrate safety and efficacy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In this 2020 first-in-human (early feasibility) multicenter, phase 1, open prospective cohort study, (Paulus et al.,
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2020) the authors assessed the safety of the Exilis™ gastric electrical stimulation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They also sought to investigate whether the settings can be adjusted for comfortable chronic use in Class II or III obese patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Meal intake and gastric emptying and motility were also evaluated.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In this study, 20 obese patients were implanted with the Exilis system and amplitude was individually set during 4 amplitude titration visits.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Subjects underwent two blinded baseline test days (GES ON vs. OFF), after which long-term, monthly follow-up continued for up to 52 weeks.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results suggested that this device is safe and caused no patient discomfort.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: At baseline food intake and satiety were not significantly different when the device was on or off, and significant weight loss occurred at week 26, with EWL of 14% at 52 weeks.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors conclude that the data were comparable with studies of subjects on diet and/or exercise alone, but disappointing when compared to minimally invasive procedures, such as gastric banding or endoscopic gastroplication.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Furthermore, the authors did not observe changes in plasma glucose and insulin levels which other bariatric procedures are known to improve.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that considerably more basic research is required before clinical use.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Limitations included small sample size, lack of control group, and lack of long-term outcomes.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a in a 12-month prospective multicenter study, Morales-Conde et al. (
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2018) monitored all participants (n = 47) up to 24 months after laparoscopic implantation of a closed-loop GES system (CLGES).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Weight loss, safety, quality of life (QOL), and cardiac risk factors were analyzed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Weight regain was limited in the 35 (74%) participants remaining enrolled at 24 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Mean %TBWL changed by only 1.5% between 12 and 24 months, reported at 14.8% (95% CI 12.3 to 17.3) and 13.3% (95% CI 10.7 to 15.8), respectively.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The only serious device-/procedure-related AEs were two elective system replacements due to lead failure in the first 12 months, while improvements in QOL and cardiovascular risk factors were stable thru 24 months.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors conclude that during the 24-month follow-up, CLGES was shown to limit weight regain with strong safety outcomes, including no serious AEs in the second year.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: They hypothesize that CLGES and objective sensor-based behavior data combined to produce behavior change, and in their opinion supports GES as a safe obesity treatment with potential for long-term health benefits.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Larger well-designed randomized controlled trials are needed to further evaluate the safety and efficacy of GES therapy in the treatment of obesity.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: In a post-implant analysis, Alarcón Del Agua I, et al. (
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: 2017) evaluated possible preoperative predictors for obtaining clinically meaningful weight loss with GES.
